text:
<@UJBAJNFLK>'s link <https://feelingofcomputing.slack.com/archives/C5U3SEW6A/p1776099082547179> spawned this thought, among others, as I watched:

> **TL;DR** — Old code is dying. The code isn't the problem. The business rules embedded in it are. LLMs can extract those rules into a factbase. A factbase can be rewritten into any target language. This is Design Recovery, and it is the right job for LLMs.
<https://open.substack.com/pub/programmingsimplicity/p/llms-as-design-recovery-engines?r=1egdky&utm_campaign=post&utm_medium=web&showWelcomeOnShare=true|LLMs as Design Recovery Engines>

elements.text: with Paul Rony, on lessons from the history of computing:

elements.text: spawned this thought, among others, as I watched:

* — Old code is dying. The code isn't the problem. The business rules embedded in it are. LLMs can extract those rules into a factbase. A factbase can be rewritten into any target language. This is Design Recovery, and it is the right job for LLMs.

text:
Some thoughts about <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_dhsm0PLOk0|The Computing Historian Recovering Ideas We Threw Away>

• we finally have asynchronous multi-tasking - i.e. the internet - many independent computers, running at their own speed
• we have some of the same “concurrency” issues, with internet, yet simpler, since internet does not allow actual memory sharing (you can share data, but not memory)
• modern computing has been overrun by technical/academic complication, not by UX simplicity
• the “new” invention of CompSci is _not_ “programming” but “re-programming”
• Design Recovery to lift designs out of old software and to re-express them in code for new RMs - what is the meta-language? (factbases of triples?)
<https://programmingsimplicity.substack.com/p/some-thoughts-about-the-computing?r=1egdky|...>

we finally have asynchronous multi-tasking - i.e. the internet - many independent computers, running at their own speed
we have some of the same “concurrency” issues, with internet, yet simpler, since internet does not allow actual memory sharing (you can share data, but not memory)
modern computing has been overrun by technical/academic complication, not by UX simplicity
Design Recovery to lift designs out of old software and to re-express them in code for new RMs - what is the meta-language? (factbases of triples?)

text:
Running coding agents backwards is a possibility. But not a trivial problem to solve. The "factbase" is what, exactly? For the code I deal with, it would have to include mathematical relations between numbers. The code being an approximation (floats etc) to the relations that one would like to see recovered. Not impossible, given that the number of numerical algorithms is limited and an LLM could have seen them all. But the structure of the factbase is part of the design that needs to be recovered. I don't know if an LLM can deal with that.

And then there is the eternal question with LLMs: do you trust the output to be correct, or at least good enough? Much of the legacy code we are using has no tests, no specification, etc. It's just the code, plus input/output combinations (which are easy to produce).

For the code I deal with, it would have to include mathematical relations between numbers. The code being an approximation (floats etc) to the relations that one would like to see recovered. Not impossible, given that the number of numerical algorithms is limited and an LLM could have seen them all. But the structure of the factbase is part of the design that needs to be recovered. I don't know if an LLM can deal with that.

And then there is the eternal question with LLMs: do you trust the output to be correct, or at least good enough? Much of the legacy code we are using has no tests, no specification, etc. It's just the code, plus input/output combinations (which are easy to produce).
